User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the LexManager application, typically an attorney or legal assistant, managing legal processes, clients, and documents. This entity facilitates tracking who made modifications.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: Email address of the user, used for communication.

role.description: The role of the user within the system (e.g., 'Attorney', 'Paralegal', 'Admin').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Client.title: Client
Client.description: Stores detailed information about a client, including contact details, case history, and billing information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity.

name.description: Full name of the client or the company name.

contactPerson.description: The primary contact person for the client, if applicable (e.g., for a corporate client).

email.description: Primary email address of the client.

phone.description: Primary phone number of the client.

address.description: Full physical address of the client.

caseHistorySummary.description: A summary or overview of the client's past legal case history.

billingDetails.description: General description or summary of billing arrangements for the client.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the client record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the client record was last updated.

createdBy.description: Reference to the User who created this client record. (Relationship: User 1:N Client)

updatedBy.description: Reference to the User who last updated this client record. (Relationship: User 1:N Client)

LegalProcess.title: LegalProcess
LegalProcess.description: Tracks key information for a specific legal process or case, including its status, dates, and related parties.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LegalProcess entity.

title.description: The title or a brief descriptive name of the legal process/case.

description.description: A detailed description of the legal process, including its background and objectives.

caseNumber.description: An internal or external unique case number for the legal process.

status.description: The current status of the legal process (e.g., 'Open', 'Pending', 'Closed', 'Appealed').

startDate.description: The date when the legal process officially commenced.

dueDate.description: The primary overall deadline for the completion of the legal process.

clientId.description: Reference to the Client associated with this legal process. (Relationship: Client 1:N LegalProcess)

responsibleUserId.description: Reference to the User primarily responsible for managing this legal process. (Relationship: User 1:N LegalProcess)

tagIds.description: References to Tags applied to this legal process for categorization. (Relationship: Tag N:N LegalProcess)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the legal process record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the legal process record was last updated.

createdBy.description: Reference to the User who created this legal process record. (Relationship: User 1:N LegalProcess)

updatedBy.description: Reference to the User who last updated this legal process record. (Relationship: User 1:N LegalProcess)

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Manages information about uploaded documents pertinent to legal processes or clients, including file details and associated tags.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

fileName.description: The original name of the uploaded file.

fileUrl.description: The URL or path where the document is stored.

fileType.description: The MIME type or file extension of the document (e.g., 'application/pdf', 'image/jpeg').

description.description: A brief description or summary of the document's content.

uploadDate.description: The date and time when the document was uploaded.

legalProcessId.description: Reference to the LegalProcess this document is associated with. (Relationship: LegalProcess 1:N Document)

clientId.description: Reference to the Client this document is associated with. (Relationship: Client 1:N Document)

tagIds.description: References to Tags applied to this document for categorization. (Relationship: Tag N:N Document)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the document record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the document record was last updated.

uploadedBy.description: Reference to the User who uploaded this document. (Relationship: User 1:N Document)

updatedBy.description: Reference to the User who last updated this document record. (Relationship: User 1:N Document)

Tag.title: Tag
Tag.description: Defines categories or keywords for organizing and searching legal processes and documents.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Tag entity.

description.description: A short description explaining the purpose or typical usage of the tag.

Deadline.title: Deadline
Deadline.description: Represents specific dates and times for important events, tasks, or deadlines within a legal process, used for tracking and reminders.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Deadline entity.

title.description: A concise title for the deadline or event.

description.description: A detailed description of the deadline or event, including any necessary context or instructions.

dateTime.description: The exact date and time of the deadline or event.

type.description: The category or type of the event/deadline (e.g., 'Court Hearing', 'Filing Deadline', 'Client Meeting').

status.description: The current status of the deadline (e.g., 'Upcoming', 'Completed', 'Missed', 'Rescheduled').

legalProcessId.description: Reference to the LegalProcess this deadline belongs to. (Relationship: LegalProcess 1:N Deadline)

remindMe.description: Indicates whether a reminder should be generated for this deadline.

reminderSentAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the reminder for this deadline was last sent, if applicable.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the deadline record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the deadline record was last updated.

createdBy.description: Reference to the User who created this deadline record. (Relationship: User 1:N Deadline)

updatedBy.description: Reference to the User who last updated this deadline record. (Relationship: User 1:N Deadline)

Alert.title: Alert
Alert.description: Stores AI-generated alerts or reminders regarding new rules, regulations, or precedents applicable to legal processes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Alert entity.

title.description: A concise title summarizing the alert.

description.description: A detailed explanation of the new rule, regulation, or precedent and its potential applicability.

type.description: The category of the alert (e.g., 'Rule Change', 'Precedent Update', 'Regulatory Notice').

source.description: The origin of the information (e.g., 'Supreme Court Ruling', 'New Regulation X', 'Legal Journal').

alertDate.description: The date and time when the alert was generated or identified.

legalProcessIds.description: References to LegalProcess entities that this alert is applicable to. (Relationship: LegalProcess N:N Alert)

userId.description: Reference to the User (attorney) for whom this alert is primarily intended. (Relationship: User 1:N Alert)

isRead.description: Indicates whether the alert has been acknowledged or read by the recipient.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the alert record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the alert record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores user profiles and roles. The 'role' field is used for Database Access Control (DBAC). Documents are accessible only by the user whose ID matches '{userId}' (i.e., `request.auth.uid == userId`).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores client records, privately owned by the attorney specified by '{userId}'. The 'createdBy' field in the document must match '{userId}' for authorization independence. Client data is accessible only by the owning attorney.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the attorney (user) who owns this client record.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique identifier for the client.

definition.description: Tracks legal processes, nested under a specific client and owned by the attorney '{userId}'. The 'responsibleUserId' and 'createdBy' fields in the document must match '{userId}', and 'clientId' must match '{clientId}' for authorization independence. Access is restricted to the owning attorney.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the attorney (user) who owns this legal process.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the client associated with this legal process.

params.name: legalProcessId
params.description: The unique identifier for the legal process.

definition.description: Manages documents related to a legal process, nested under its parent legal process, client, and owner attorney. The 'uploadedBy' field must match '{userId}', 'clientId' must match '{clientId}', and 'legalProcessId' must match '{legalProcessId}' for authorization independence. Access is restricted to the owning attorney.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the attorney (user) who uploaded and owns this document.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the client associated with this document.

params.name: legalProcessId
params.description: The unique identifier of the legal process this document belongs to.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the document.

definition.description: Stores specific deadlines and events for a legal process, nested under its parent legal process, client, and owner attorney. The 'createdBy' field must match '{userId}', 'clientId' must be denormalized into the document and match '{clientId}', and 'legalProcessId' must match '{legalProcessId}' for authorization independence. Access is restricted to the owning attorney.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the attorney (user) who created and owns this deadline.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the client associated with this deadline.

params.name: legalProcessId
params.description: The unique identifier of the legal process this deadline belongs to.

params.name: deadlineId
params.description: The unique identifier for the deadline.

definition.description: Contains AI-generated alerts intended for a specific attorney, directly owned by the user. The 'userId' field in the document must match '{userId}' for authorization independence. Alerts are only accessible by the intended recipient attorney.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the attorney (user) for whom this alert is intended.

params.name: alertId
params.description: The unique identifier for the alert.

definition.description: This collection serves as a global dictionary for tags. Read access is typically allowed for all authenticated users, while modification access is usually restricted to users with 'Admin' roles (e.g., by checking `/users/{uid}.role`).

params.name: tagId
params.description: The unique identifier for the tag.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for LexManager prioritizes Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation to ensure robust, debuggable security rules and efficient queries (QAPs). 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
All user-specific data (Clients, Legal Processes, Documents, Deadlines, and Alerts) is nested under the `/users/{userId}` path. This establishes a clear ownership hierarchy where the `{userId}` in the path directly corresponds to the owner/manager of the nested data. To eliminate the need for `get()` operations in security rules, crucial authorization fields are denormalized directly into each document:
*   **Client:** The `createdBy` field must match the `{userId}` in the path.
*   **LegalProcess:** The `responsibleUserId` and `createdBy` fields must match the `{userId}` in the path, and the `clientId` field must match the `{clientId}` in the path.
*   **Document:** The `uploadedBy` field must match the `{userId}` in the path, and both `clientId` and `legalProcessId` must match their respective path parameters. 
*   **Deadline:** The `createdBy` field must match the `{userId}` in the path. Crucially, `clientId` is denormalized into the Deadline document (even though it's implicitly available from the parent LegalProcess's path) to allow direct authorization checks without fetching the LegalProcess document. The `legalProcessId` also matches its path parameter.
*   **Alert:** The `userId` field must match the `{userId}` in the path.
This denormalization strategy means that any authorization check can be performed solely using `request.auth.uid`, the path parameters (`{userId}`, `{clientId}`, etc.), and the fields within the `resource.data` of the document being accessed, thereby avoiding complex and expensive `get()` calls.

**QAPs (Query as a Proxy to Security) Support:**
Structural Segregation ensures that all documents within a given collection share a homogeneous security posture. For instance, all documents under `/users/{userId}/clients/{clientId}/legalProcesses` are privately owned by the `{userId}` and associated with the `{clientId}`. This allows for secure `list` queries. A user can query `users/{request.auth.uid}/clients` to retrieve all their clients, and Firebase's security rules can simply verify that `request.auth.uid` matches the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, and that the `createdBy` field (or `responsibleUserId`, `uploadedBy`) in the document matches this `uid`. This makes list operations secure by design, as the path itself acts as a primary filter. Global data like `Tags` resides in a separate collection (`/tags`), allowing for different, typically more permissive read-access rules (e.g., read by all authenticated users) and restricted write-access (e.g., only by 'Admin' roles, checked via a `role` field on the user's profile document `users/{request.auth.uid}.role`).
